pattern: Only the head of the surviving sequence needs tracking: each pass doubles the gap and, when it scans toward the side the head sits on, advances the head by the current step.
hints: Simulating the whole list is impossible for n up to a billion; track just which number currently leads the survivors.
Each pass halves the count and doubles the spacing between survivors; the head only moves when the pass removes the current first element.

A left-to-right pass always removes the head; a right-to-left pass removes it only when an odd count remains. Update the head by the step accordingly, double the step, halve the count, and stop at one survivor.

edge_inputs.description: two elements: first pass removes the head
odd count makes the right-to-left pass skip the head

stress.description: random n across the full range including large values
stress.generator_python:
def gen(rng, size):
    return [rng.randint(1, max(1, size))]
stress.note: the log-time head tracking handles billion-scale n that no list simulation could
